title: Return of the Jedi 1
content:

'''''Return of the Jedi'' 1''' is the first issue of the ''[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (1983)|Star Wars: Return of the Jedi]]'' mini-series and is part of the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series of comic books.

It is part one of a four issue comic mini-series adaptation of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']]. Unlike the [[Marvel Comics|Marvel comic]] adaptations of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']], this was not part of the regular series.

There are four pin-up pages in this issue, [[Bib Fortuna/Legends|Bib Fortuna]], [[Sy Snootles/Legends|Sy Snootles]] & [[Max Rebo Band/Legends|Max Rebo Band]] and [[Salacious B. Crumb/Legends|Salacious B. Crumb]] by [[Al Williamson]] and the [[Tydirium/Legends|''Tydirium'']] Shuttle by [[Rick J. Bryant]].

{{Comics-stub}}

==Publisher's summary==
''The most eagerly-awaited sequel in motion picture history is here, as '''Luke''' and Co. go running to the aid of their long-lost friend '''Han Solo'''.''<ref name="MA4" />

==Plot summary==
[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] checks on the status of the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] while the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]] attempt to rescue [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] from [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]].

==Continuity==
This issue shows [[Ree-Yees/Legends|Ree-Yees]] covering [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO's]] "mouth" to stop him from warning Luke about the trap door in [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba's]] throne room. In the movie, he instead does this to keep Threepio quiet in the earlier scene when Han and Leia are captured.
